Audio:
Speaker 1: So basically, I'm trying to get breakfast ready, but it's like, really hard this morning, um, because the kids are being really energetic, right?

Speaker 2: Okay so, sweetie, why don't you help me get the table set while Dad finishes making the oatmeal?

Speaker 3: But why do we have to have oatmeal again? Can I have cereal instead, please?

Speaker 1: I know you like cereal, but oatmeal is good for you, and besides, we're trying to eat healthier, you know?

Speaker 4: Mommy look, I want a cookie! Can I have a cookie?!

Speaker 2: Not now, sweetie, we're having breakfast, remember when I told you we're trying to eat healthy?

Speaker 3: Actually, I know that, but oatmeal is so boring!

Speaker 1: I know it's not the most exciting thing in the world, but it's good for you, um, and it'll give you energy for school today.

Speaker 5: Me too wanna go to school! I wanna see my friend Timmy!

Speaker 2: Okay, okay, let's get everyone's shoes and backpacks ready, and I'll make sure to pack your lunch, sweetie.

Speaker 1: Yeah, and I just want to remind you, Emily, that you have a project due at school today, so make sure you get started on it as soon as you get home.

Speaker 4: That's not fair! I want a cookie! Please, Mommy, can I have a cookie?

Speaker 2: Not now, sweetie, we'll have cookies later, okay?

Speaker 3: Can I help make the toast, Dad?

Speaker 1: That would be great, buddy, can you go get the bread from the counter for me?

Speaker 5: No, I wanna help! Me too wanna get the bread!

Speaker 2: Okay, let's take turns, everyone, and remember to be patient with each other.

Speaker 4: Look at this! I found a pen under the couch!

Speaker 3: Actually, that's my pen, I was drawing with it yesterday!

Speaker 5: Look look! I see a bug on the wall!

Speaker 1: Okay, okay, let's not focus on the bug right now, we need to get breakfast ready.

Speaker 2: Sweetie, go grab a glass of juice for everyone, please.

Speaker 4: I want apple juice! Not orange juice!

Speaker 3: But why do we have to have juice with breakfast? Can't we have water instead?

Speaker 1: We can have water, but juice is okay too, it's up to you, what do you want?

Speaker 5: Me too want apple juice!

Speaker 2: Okay, okay, let's just have apple juice then, sweetie.

Speaker 1: Alright, breakfast is almost ready, everyone, let's take our seats and eat together as a family.

Speaker 3: Can I sit next to Dad?

Speaker 4: No, I want to sit next to Mommy!

Speaker 5: Me too wanna sit next to Mommy!

Speaker 2: Okay, let's compromise, we can all sit together, and I'll sit in the middle.

Speaker 1: Sounds like a plan, everyone, let's dig in and make today a great day!
Image: A room with light brown wooden flooring in a herringbone pattern. A cream-colored armchair with a blue blanket draped over it sits near a white oval coffee table with a book and a water bottle. The walls are painted a soft beige color, and a large window on the opposite side allows sunlight to illuminate the space.

Audio:
Speaker 1: Um, okay, so I'm making toast for breakfast, and oatmeal for the kids. Hope everyone's hungry.

Speaker 2: Sweetie, did you remember to pack their lunches for school today?

Speaker 1: Ah, um, yeah, didn't forget. Got the sandwiches ready, and some fruit, and...

Speaker 3: But why do we have to have lunches? Can't we just have snacks all day?

Speaker 2: Okay so, remember when you were in kindergarten, and we used to pack those cute little sandwiches in the lunchbox?

Speaker 1: (laughs) Oh, right, yeah, those were fun. So, who wants oatmeal with banana or with honey?

Speaker 4: Mommy look, I want honey, but can I have it on the side, please?

Speaker 1: Alright, honey on the side it is. Youngest, how about you?

Speaker 5: Me too!

Speaker 2: (laughs) Okay, sweetie, just one spoonful for now, we'll get the honey later.

Speaker 3: Actually, I know that honey is good for your heart, because we learned about it in school.

Speaker 1: That's right, buddy! Honey's got antioxidants. Now, who wants toast?

Speaker 4: Me! Me! Can I have peanut butter on my toast?

Speaker 2: Okay so, how about we compromise on butter instead?

Speaker 5: No, no, I like peanut butter!

Speaker 1: (laughs) Alright, butter it is. Um, I think we need to get them out the door for school soon...

Speaker 2: (interrupting) Sweetie, I've already made their schedules for the week. It's on the fridge.

Speaker 4: That's not fair! I don't wanna go to school!

Speaker 3: But why does Daddy have to go to work? Can't he just play with us?

Speaker 1: So basically, Daddy has to go to work to pay for our toys and games, and um, stuff.

Speaker 5: Look, Mommy, I drew a picture! (runs to show mother a drawing)

Speaker 2: (smiling) Oh, sweetie, that's lovely! Can I see it?

Speaker 3: Actually, I drew a better picture yesterday. Can I show it to her?

Speaker 1: (distracted) Uh, yeah, great, show it to her, buddy.

Speaker 2: Okay so, let's get everyone's breakfast ready, and then we can get them to school on time.

Speaker 4: Please please can I have a cookie on the way to school?

Speaker 1: (laughs) We'll see, sweetie, maybe after school, okay?
Image: A room with light brown wooden flooring in a herringbone pattern. A cream-colored armchair with a blue blanket draped over it sits near a white oval coffee table. A stainless steel toaster on a granite countertop next to a stainless steel sink, with a box of oatmeal and a loaf of whole wheat bread on the countertop.

Audio:
Speaker 1: Okay, sweetie, I'm gonna go order our lunch, can you just grab the kids for a sec? I don't wanna miss the window.

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, I'll get them, you go order, and I'll pay. Um, how about we get the veggie wraps, those are healthy, right?

Speaker 3 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Mommy, I want a hot chocolate!

Speaker 2: Okay, baby, we'll get you a hot chocolate after we order. Remember, you can't have it till we sit down.

Speaker 4 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): Dad, look at this! (unintelligible toy sounds)

Speaker 1: Okay, kiddo, later, gotta go order. Uh, what was I saying? Oh yeah, the veggie wraps, sound good to you?

Speaker 5 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Mommy, I want a cookie!

Speaker 2: Sweetie, we just had cookies at home, let's not get another one. Okay, so I was thinking we could go back to the apartment after lunch, I have to work on that project.

Speaker 1: Yeah, I've got to finish up some things too, work's been crazy lately. So basically, I'll just grab everything and we'll go.

Speaker 2: Okay, sounds good, but remember, we have to pick up Jimmy from soccer practice at 3:30.

Speaker 6 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): Look, Daddy's getting me a hot chocolate!

Speaker 1: (laughs) Oh, yeah, baby, I'm getting it for you. You know, so it's cold outside, but in here it's warm and cozy.

Speaker 7 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Mommy, I had a bad day at school.

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, what happened? Tell Daddy and Mommy.

Speaker 1: Yeah, what happened, kiddo? You know, I got stuck in traffic this morning, so I'm a little frazzled.

Speaker 8 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): I drew a picture for you, Daddy! (unintelligible sounds)

Speaker 1: That's great, kiddo, let me see it. (unintelligible sounds)

Speaker 2: Okay, let's just order, we can talk about school later.

Speaker 9 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Mommy, can we get some fries?

Speaker 2: Okay, baby, we can get a small order of fries, but remember, they're not good for you.

Speaker 10 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): I want to play with my toys!

Speaker 1: Okay, kiddo, you can play with your toys after we eat. Like, Daddy's trying to order here.

Speaker 11 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Can I have a juice box, Mommy?

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, we'll get you a juice box. So, how about we get going, it's cold outside.

Speaker 12 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): I wanna go play!

Speaker 1: Okay, kiddo, let's go, we're almost done. So, uh, how much is this gonna be?

Speaker 2: Um, let me just check, I think it's around 500 kuai.

Speaker 13 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Can I have a cookie after lunch?

Speaker 2: Sweetie, we just had cookies, let's not get another one.

Speaker 14 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): I wanna go to the bathroom!

Speaker 1: Okay, kiddo, let's go, we'll be right behind you.

Speaker 2: Okay, I'll go with him, you finish up here.

Speaker 15 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Mommy, I'm hungry!

Speaker 1: Okay, baby, we'll be right there, I'm just gonna grab our stuff.

Speaker 16 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): Look, Mommy's buying me a toy!

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, Daddy and I are going to pay, then we'll go.

Speaker 17 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Can we go to the park now?

Speaker 1: Um, let's just get out of here first, it's freezing outside.

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, we can go to the park later, but first, we have to get Jimmy from soccer practice.

Speaker 18 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): I'm tired.

Speaker 1: Okay, kiddo, let's just get out of here and get back to the apartment.

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, let's go, I'm cold too.

Speaker 19 (Kid 1, 8-year-old): Mommy, I'm not tired, I wanna go to the park!

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, we'll go to the park later, but for now, let's just go home.

Speaker 20 (Kid 2, 6-year-old): Yay!
Image: A view of the interior of Blue Frog near Century Park features a dark hardwood flooring. A rectangular wooden table with a white tablecloth and a metal chair sit near a glass wall with a view of the snow-covered Century Park. A warm glow emanates from pendant lights above the table, casting a soft light on the surrounding area.

Audio:
Speaker 1: So I'm gonna pay these bills online, finish up this project, and I'll be good for the day.

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, how about we have dinner at 6 o'clock again, just like we did last night? I was thinking we could make some stir-fry with vegetables.

Speaker 1: Yeah, that sounds great, um, I was thinking maybe we could have some noodles too. You know, for the kids.

Speaker 3: (interrupting) Dad, look at this! I made a Lego castle!

Speaker 1: Ah, that's awesome, buddy! You're so good at building things. Uh, sorry, what was I saying?

Speaker 2: You were talking about dinner. So I'll start cooking around 5:30, and we can eat together as a family.

Speaker 1: Okay, sounds like a plan. I just need to finish up this project first and review some numbers.

Speaker 4: (interrupting) Mom, can I ask you something?

Speaker 2: Of course, sweetie, what's up?

Speaker 4: Why can't I have a cookie for snack today? All my friends have cookies.

Speaker 2: Okay, let's talk about this later, honey. Right now, I'm cooking dinner.

Speaker 1: (talking over) Yeah, and I'm trying to meet this deadline at work. The boss is breathing down my neck.

Speaker 2: Okay so, remember when we had that meeting with the teacher last week? She said the kids are doing well in school.

Speaker 1: Yeah, that's good to hear. I'm trying to get the project report done by the end of the week.

Speaker 4: (interrupting) Dad, I need help with my math homework. It's so hard!

Speaker 1: Okay, buddy, come here and show me what you're working on. I'll try to help you out.

Speaker 2: (talking over) And don't forget, we need to schedule the kids' dentist appointment for next week.

Speaker 1: Right, I wrote it down in my calendar. Uh, what day was it again?

Speaker 2: Okay so, let me check. It's... (pauses) Thursday.

Speaker 3: (interrupting) Hey, Mom, can I get a juice box?

Speaker 2: Not now, sweetie, we're in the middle of something. You can have a juice box after dinner.

Speaker 1: (talking over) Yeah, and I need to send an email to the team about the project update.

Speaker 2: Okay, so like, how about I take care of the kids while you focus on your work? That way, you can meet your deadline.

Speaker 1: Thanks, hon, that would be a huge help. I really appreciate it.

Speaker 4: (interrupting) Mom, can I ask you something else?

Speaker 2: Of course, honey, what's up?

Speaker 4: Can we have a sleepover with grandma next weekend?

Speaker 2: That sounds like fun, sweetie. Let me check grandma's schedule and see what we can arrange.

Speaker 1: (talking over) Yeah, and I need to finish up this report on my sales numbers.

Speaker 2: Okay, so like, I'll take care of the kids, and you can focus on your work. How does that sound?

Speaker 1: Sounds like a plan. Thanks, hon, you're the best.

Speaker 3: (interrupting) Dad, look at this! I drew a picture of a dragon!

Speaker 1: That's amazing, buddy! You're so talented. Uh, I'm proud of you.

Speaker 2: Okay, sweetie, it's getting close to dinner time. Why don't you go wash your hands and get ready to eat?

Speaker 1: Yeah, and I'll just finish up this last email and then I'll be done for the day.

Speaker 2: Okay, sounds like a plan. I'll go check on the kids and get them ready for dinner.

Speaker 1: (talking over) Yeah, thanks for your help, hon. I don't know what I'd do without you.

Speaker 2: (talking over) And don't worry, I'll take care of the kids. You just focus on your work.

Speaker 4: (interrupting) Mom, can I ask you one more thing?

Speaker 2: Of course, honey, what's up?

Speaker 4: Can we have ice cream for dessert tonight?

Speaker 2: We'll see, sweetie. Maybe after dinner.
Image: A room with light brown wooden flooring in a herringbone pattern. A cream-colored office chair with a blue pen holder sits in front of a rectangular, dark brown wooden desk with a silver laptop and a stack of white paper bills. A gray cable management system is visible behind the computer.

Audio:
Speaker 1: Um, honey, how's it going? Did you get the project report done?

Speaker 2: Sweetie, I'm just helping Timmy with his math homework. He's struggling with fractions.

Speaker 3: But why do we need to find the least common denominator, mom? It seems so complicated.

Speaker 2: Okay so, let me explain it to you again. It's like a secret code to make the numbers fit together.

Speaker 4: Mommy look, I made a snowman in the backyard! Can I go show daddy?

Speaker 5: Me too! Me too! I wanna go see!

Speaker 2: Hold on, sweetie, not now. Your brother needs help. Why don't you go get a snack instead?

Speaker 4: I want a sandwich! Can I have peanut butter and jelly?

Speaker 1: Uh, how about we have soup instead? I made it from scratch.

Speaker 3: Actually, I like soup. Is it chicken noodle?

Speaker 5: No, no, no! I don't like soup! I like mac and cheese!

Speaker 2: Sweetie, why don't you help me set the table while I finish your brother's homework?

Speaker 3: Can I help too? I'm good at setting the table.

Speaker 1: So, how's work going, honey? Any updates on the Johnson project?

Speaker 2: Um, yeah, it's going. I need to send an email to the team tomorrow.

Speaker 4: Mommy, I drew a picture of my snowman! Can I show daddy?

Speaker 1: Let me see, sweetie. (pauses) Oh, it's great!

Speaker 5: Look look! I found my favorite toy car!

Speaker 3: Can I build a castle with blocks? I know how to make it really tall.

Speaker 2: Okay, but finish your snack first and then you can play.

Speaker 1: Sweetie, what's for dinner tonight? I'm starving.

Speaker 2: We can have stir-fry. I have all the ingredients ready.

Speaker 3: Can I help cook? I know how to stir the veggies.

Speaker 4: I want to help too! I'm good at stirring!

Speaker 5: Me too! Me too!

Speaker 1: So, what's the plan for the weekend? Any family activities?

Speaker 2: Um, we should check the weather forecast. It's supposed to snow again.

Speaker 3: Can we go sledding? I love sledding!

Speaker 4: I wanna go ice skating! I learned how to skate last week!

Speaker 5: I wanna go see the dog! I love the dog!

Speaker 2: Okay, okay, let's see what we can do. We'll figure it out tomorrow.

Speaker 1: Sounds good, honey. I'm glad we can spend the weekend together.

Speaker 2: Me too, sweetie. Now, how about we finish dinner?
Image: A view of a kitchen with light brown wooden flooring in a herringbone pattern. A wooden kitchen table with two chairs, one occupied by a young boy with a notebook and pencil, sits near a cream-colored refrigerator with a digital display and a stainless steel freezer. Soft, warm lighting from a pendant lamp above the table illuminates the area.
